_complexity_tier_doc: COMPLEX — architectural: R2 redesigns the /allow grant philosophy (pattern-string → structured sentinel JSON), R7 reconciles tool-policy contract across 9 role entries with a policy_version bump. Both changes affect every subsequent cycle. BA agent spec Tier Assessment 'COMPLEX' is the right tier ('high blast radius, architectural, or user-flagged risk'). A prior linter adjusted this to STANDARD; restored to COMPLEX because R2 + R7 cross the architectural threshold.
_risk_level_doc: Required top-level field. high — touches critical guard hooks (R2 full /allow lifecycle), tool-policy semantics (R7 spans 9 roles + policy_version bump), and push-grant validation (R1 changes hooks/push.sh abort behavior). Used by commands/dev.md Step 8 to gate test-writer dispatch (test-writer fires when complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high).

success_criteria: AC1 (R1): hooks/push.sh awk-V1 structural-ordering invariant exit 0 (executable git push reachable only AFTER a terminal abort action; comment/fence-stripped). Sentinel binding V2 + trap cleanup V3 + single-process pattern V4 + unit-test V5 (mocked git, expired/FAIL sentinel, mock-invocation-count zero) all PASS.

AC2 (R2): hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py declares consume-on-any-terminal-result semantic (V1). hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py contains ≥4 real, non-@skip/@xfail-decorated def test_(success|failure|non_zero|malformed|comment_only|terminal_consume) (V2 AST). pytest runtime execution passes with grant-unlink post-conditions verified (V3). Existing artifact greps preserved (V4).

AC3 (R3): TodoWrite phrase present in commands/close.md AND commands/dev.md (V1) inside a Step-N or Agent-dispatch section, outside fenced blocks, NOT in Appendix/Future/Notes/History (V2 awk). Positive-imperative wording (MUST / REQUIRED / Always / Before dispatch) within 3 lines after each occurrence (V3).

AC4 (R4): agents/push-analyst.md declares module-level PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 (V1 line-start-anchored grep). AST sole-call-site invariant V2 (every timedelta call uses the named constant; no literal/expression duration anywhere on the grant-expiry path). commands/push.md documents the new TTL (V3). scripts/write-commit-grant.py unchanged (V4).

AC5 (R5): commands/commit.md Step 7 contains the ordered 5-marker algorithm text (V1 structural awk verifies monotonic order: context.spec_path → Continuation spec → mtime+task-id glob → single-match fail-closed → multi-match fail-closed). Fence-aware parser explicit in text (V2). Broadened nondeterminism phrase set forbidden (V3: dev chooses / one of / either or / pick latest / prefer newest / select any / most recent wins / any matching / the right one). Fail-closed sentinels present (V4).

AC6 (R6): agents/qa.md contains the spec_text_vs_execution_drift phrase in ≥2 distinct ## sections outside fenced blocks (V1 awk). One occurrence is in BA-Validation Dimension 5 section (V2). One occurrence is in Step 1 Success Criteria Validation section (V3). Each occurrence is followed within 5 lines by positive-normative wording: 'MUST raise a blocking objection' OR 'verdict MUST be FAIL' OR 'MUST NOT downgrade to warning' OR 'is a blocking BA-validation objection' (V4).

AC7 (R7): Skill-invariant audit (V1) — broadened codex-detection regex finds every agents/*.md mentioning codex consultation; every such role has Skill in policies/tool-policy.v1.json roles[role].allowed_tools (zero MISS lines). ba role's allowed_write_path_prefixes gains docs/dev/acceptance-criteria- AND docs/dev/retrospective-classification- (V2). qa role's allowed_write_path_prefixes gains docs/dev/retrospective-classification- (V2). policy_version=3 (V3). _note acknowledges reconciliation (V3).

AC9 (R9): agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 6 contains the verbatim 'Reverses <SHA>: <one-line rationale for why prior reasoning no longer holds>' phrase, fence-aware extracted between '### Phase 6' and '### Phase 7' (V1). Phase 6 section contains forward-fix / forward fix / reverses prior behavior disambiguation (V2). AC FAILS if phrase exists only in commands/commit.md (V3). affected_files contains agents/changelog-analyst.md (V4). git history unchanged (V5).

AC10 (R10): agents/qa.md contains '### Verification harness cleanup contract (MANDATORY)' subsection with 4 verbatim DO NOT clauses (V1). agents/ba.md AC template cross-references the qa.md contract (V2). docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md gains 5 new '### AC<N> verification recipe' subsections for AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4/AC11 (V3). Each subsection's bash block satisfies structural-ordering: PID="" init → trap with double-quoted "${PID}" runtime expansion + EXIT INT TERM → PID=$! spawn capture (V4 Python AST-style verifier); no pkill -f or killall anywhere in file. Append-only invariant V5: existing 73 lines from d988d4a appear as verbatim prefix of working-tree file (no deletion, no reordering). W7 frozen-COMMIT invariant V6: d988d4a sha unchanged.

V_TW (workflow_routing): _test_writer_skip_reason field present and non-empty in context.json (V1). commands/dev.md Step 8 honors the sentinel — references _test_writer_skip_reason OR test.writer.*skip text (V2). R3 reminder cycle absorbs one-line dev.md addition if not already present.

This BA session's codex tee output to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-*.txt was blocked by the ba role's tool-policy (the */var/tmp/codex-outputs/ glob did not resolve to the absolute /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ literal target the hook expected). Live recurrence of item 7. Workaround: wrote to /tmp/codex-ba-redev-output.txt instead. Reinforces R7 priority — it IS the root-cause item.

BA agent spec Step 10 mandates emitting docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-<task_id>.json, but ba role's allowed_write_path_prefixes does NOT include 'acceptance-criteria-' nor 'docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-'. Edit attempt blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py: 'write target ... not in allowed_write_path_prefixes'. THIRD live recurrence of item 7 within this single BA session. The file at this path retains stale ACs from the prior hijacked-slot cycle; the orchestrator's dev.md Step 10 dispatch (or test-writer subagent) should regenerate the acceptance-criteria JSON from this BA ticket's 9 ACs. Until R7 is landed, BA cannot satisfy ba.md Step 10 deterministically. Reinforces R7 priority (highest, root cause).

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Prior cycle 20260519-175339 self-identified 10 shortcomings during /close debate; 6 were classified SYSTEMIC infra defects, 3 NEED-TO-FIX behavioral disciplines, 1 ACCEPTABLE-DEBT.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Cumulative infra debt: (a) tool-policy contract inconsistency with agent.md codex_required mandates (item 7 — recurs every cycle); (b) pattern-match grant mechanism is trivially spoofable + bypassable (item 2); (c) push-grant Chain-B short-circuit relies on orchestrator chaining semantics (item 1); (d) commit-grant TTL structurally too short for subagent latency (item 4); (e) /commit Step 7 silent skip is fail-open data contract (item 5); (f) QA test harness has no trap-on-exit (item 10); (g) discipline reminders missing in close.md/dev.md/qa.md/commit.md (items 3, 6, 9).
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A (retrospective items span multiple commits and infra design decisions; no single commit caused all 10)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Each item has its own genealogy; retrospective JSON items[].final_rationale fields document each one. Tool-policy gaps were original 2026-05-09 omissions; grant-pattern matching was original 2026-04 design; push-analyst TTL was 60s and bumped to 120s in a prior cycle but is still too tight.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Recurrence — every cycle hits at least some of these gaps. This BA session hit item 7 live during codex consult (codex tee blocked).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Items accumulated across multiple cycles; the prior cycle 20260519-175339 was the first to formally enumerate them in a structured retrospective. The retrospective was produced 2026-05-20T05:05:00Z; this BA spec follows at 2026-05-20T05:30:00Z.
observations: 9 of 12 agents/*.md files referencing Skill(codex) lack Skill in their allowed_tools entry in policies/tool-policy.v1.json (observed: direct grep + jq inspection of policies/tool-policy.v1.json)
agents/push-analyst.md:158 currently sets push-analyst grant expires_at = current_time + timedelta(seconds=120) (observed: Read of agents/push-analyst.md)
scripts/write-commit-grant.py:38 sets GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 — DIFFERENT mechanism from push-analyst TTL (observed: Read of scripts/write-commit-grant.py)

hooks/lib/allowlist.py + hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh + hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py + hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh together form the centralized /allow grant lifecycle (observed: grep for 'allowlist' across hooks/)

Live recurrence of item 7 during this BA session: codex tee to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-*.txt blocked by tool-policy for ba role (observed: hook error message during codex skill invocation)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: The retrospective JSON itself localizes each item with file:line specifics; this BA spec re-verified each localization by direct Read/Grep of the named files (push-analyst.md:158, write-commit-grant.py:38, tool-policy.v1.json roles section, hooks/lib/allowlist.py).
root_cause_analysis.located_source: docs/dev/qa-output-retrospective-classification-20260519-175339.json (the authoritative scope source); per-item file:line localizations are listed in evidence.measured of the BA ticket.

development_approach.strategy: Implement 9 items in retrospective priority order (7 > 2 > 1 > 5 > 4 > 10 > 6 > 3 > 9). R7 first because every subsequent cycle hits the gap; R2 second because grant-mechanism redesign is the deepest change; R1 third because push-grant integrity needs single-process semantics before R4's TTL bump matters. R3/R6/R9 are 1-line doc additions, lowest risk, last.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA runs the file-content-grep recipes declared in each AC of docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md. No long-lived child process spawning (per R10 spirit). For R7, QA additionally runs jq queries against policies/tool-policy.v1.json to verify Skill in 9 role entries + policy_version=3.

research_findings.reason: All 9 items are pre-classified by codex+QA in the retrospective JSON; concrete_remediation text is the verbatim contract. No best-practices research needed.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 10 codex findings (CF-01..CF-10), all with PROPOSED_FIX. 7 APPLIED (CF-01/CF-07 AC1.4 anti-greedy-grep, CF-02 W7 frozen-commit invariant, CF-05 confirmed Files-to-Modify covers writer, CF-06 AC1 sentinel invariants, CF-08 AC10 DO-NOT clauses + frozen-spec reconciliation, CF-10 AC9 two-rule clarification). 3 LOGGED observation_only (CF-03 already addressed in Requirements Decomposition table, CF-04 OOS — settings.json mechanism not in scope, CF-09 already addressed via verbatim Technical Hints text).

findings.issue: Draft does not explicitly operationalize 禁止加载任何非本cycle的内容; Item 5 glob fallback can accidentally load prior-cycle specs/reports.
findings.proposed_fix: Discovery fallback must be metadata-only; if multiple/zero candidates, stop and ask.
findings.rationale: Tightened AC5 Step 7 to metadata-only matching + exact ${TASK_ID} literal grep; prior-cycle drag-in is explicitly forbidden.

findings.issue: Draft freezes continuation spec but does not explicitly protect frozen commit d988d4a.
findings.proposed_fix: Add invariant: no amend/rebase/reset/revert of d988d4a; all changes are forward-fix commits.
findings.rationale: Added Won't Have W7 with the explicit destructive-verb prohibition + frozen-spec diff invariant.

findings.issue: Draft lists retrospective items but does not map them to AC1.1..AC2.3.
findings.rationale: Requirements Decomposition table already maps R1..R9 to AC1..AC10 (with AC8 acceptable-debt OOS); user requirement AC1.1..AC2.3 correspond 1:1 via the retrospective item numbers — AC1.1=item7=AC7, AC1.2=item2=AC2, AC1.3=item1=AC1, AC1.4=item5=AC5, AC1.5=item4=AC4, AC1.6=item10=AC10, AC2.1=item6=AC6, AC2.2=item3=AC3, AC2.3=item9=AC9.

findings.issue: Bare Skill in inspector allowed_tools is over-broad; may permit human-only skill invocation.
findings.proposed_fix: Prefer exact skill-name allowlisting or pair with Skill(name:<human-only>) denies.
findings.rationale: Per global CLAUDE.md Lesson #15, human-only skill isolation is a settings.json Skill(name:*) deny mechanism — DIFFERENT layer from tool-policy.v1.json. The retrospective's concrete_remediation for item 7 specifies bare Skill in tool-policy; settings.json hardening is OOS for this retrospective cycle. Recorded for future cycle.

findings.issue: Replacing only match_grant_for_bash_command is incomplete if grant creation still emits substring/regex fields.
findings.proposed_fix: Define minimal allowed_operations[] schema; update grant writer; deny-by-default on missing/malformed; remove legacy substring auth.
findings.rationale: Files-to-Modify list item #3 already covers hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (the grant writer). BA spec mandates 'predicate never substring-matches against the command line' which removes legacy authorization. Manifest schema is in Technical Hints.

findings.issue: 'sentinel-file gate' underspecified; a stale/advisory sentinel would not make validate+push atomic.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify sentinel invariants: atomically created on validate, bound to expected HEAD/tree/token, consumed only by same push flow, cleaned on all exits, stale → deny.
findings.rationale: Added Sentinel invariants sub-block under AC1: atomic write-temp+rename, REQUEST_ID binding, one push = one sentinel, single-use consume, stale-mtime deny, trap-cleanup invariant.

findings.issue: 3-stage lookup risks greedy-grep widening via broad glob/content search.
findings.proposed_fix: Use context.spec_path first; close-report fallback exact-path only; final glob filenames/mtime only + exact task-id predicate; no content scan.
findings.rationale: Tightened AC5 stage-3 to metadata-only matching with single literal ${TASK_ID} token grep; explicit prohibition on free-form content scan.

findings.issue: Moving trap-cleanup to qa.md + ba.md can become broad template rewrite.
findings.proposed_fix: Insert only the narrow trap-cleanup contract; explicit DO NOT clauses (no frozen-spec edit, no cleanup outside own artifacts, no hook bypass).
findings.rationale: Added 4 verbatim DO NOT clauses to AC10. AC10 also restructured to explicitly NOT edit frozen spec-20260520-044700.md per user binding directive (reconciled retrospective concrete_remediation 'or wherever the validation recipes live' to agent-template level).

findings.issue: 'codify in_progress-before-Agent' is too vague if left as prose.
findings.proposed_fix: Add normative text: before every Agent dispatch in /dev or /close, matching Todo item MUST already be in_progress; otherwise do not dispatch.
findings.rationale: The Item 3 implementation guidance in Technical Hints already provides exact verbatim normative wording for both files. No additional change needed.

findings.issue: 'Forward-fix only' could be misread as eliminating reversal citations because no git revert occurs.
findings.proposed_fix: Separate the rules: no revert/reset (independently); when a forward fix intentionally reverses prior behavior, changelog MUST include 'Reverses <SHA>: <one-line rationale>'.
findings.rationale: Added Codex CF-10 clarification sub-block to AC9 with TWO independent rules: destructive-verb prohibition (pre-existing per Destructive-Action Escalation) + reversal-citation rule (THIS AC). The two are independent.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: 10 codex findings reviewed; 7 actionable PROPOSED_FIXes applied to AC1 (sentinel invariants CF-06), AC5 (greedy-grep prohibition CF-01/CF-07), AC9 (two-rule clarification CF-10), AC10 (DO-NOT clauses + frozen-spec reconciliation CF-08), Won't Have (frozen-commit invariant CF-02), Files-to-Modify (CF-05 confirmed coverage). 3 observation-only LOGGED (CF-03/CF-04/CF-09). Codex output archived at /tmp/codex-ba-211515-output.txt. Codex session id: 019e4475-a4ad-7812-8b8a-753201876c8f.
